hook: If the date felt flat, your follow-up should still stay honest and kind.
body: Clarity after a mediocre date protects both people from unnecessary ambiguity or false hope.

hook: Men sometimes avoid sending any message because they do not want to feel like the bad guy.
body: Directness with tact is usually more respectful than fading out to avoid discomfort.

hook: Directness with tact is usually more respectful than fading out to avoid discomfort.
body: A brief note saying you enjoyed meeting but did not feel the fit can land more cleanly than silence.

body: When you know the answer is no, close the loop in a way that still respects the other person's time.
